prompt:
This is the walk-in waitlist behind the front-desk app at our vet clinic (clinic.hpp/clinic.cpp). Arrivals, ticket lookups and the next-up board have been fine for months. The trouble started when reception began using the hold sweep for owners who step out — every visit gets a hold-until time and the sweep is supposed to clear the lapsed ones.

Tuesday's board, 100% reproducible (same six arrivals, sweep at minute 40, three holds lapsed by then):

- Two of the lapsed holds happened to be neighbors in the queue, and one of those two was STILL on the board after the sweep. The sweep also reported it dropped 2 when three holds had lapsed, so the daily count in the report is off too.
- The desk lookups went sideways after the sweep: calling up T-104 displayed Clover's chart — Clover is a completely different dog whose owner never even left — and T-105 came back as 'not on the board' while Clover was sitting right there in the queue. The next-up list wanted to call a patient whose owner was gone.
- It's not tied to that exact board either: even a sweep that clears a single early hold leaves later tickets pulling up the wrong chart at the desk.

No randomness anywhere — same input, same wrong answers, every run. The whole repro is captured in test_clinic.cpp; `make test` currently fails those checks and must pass. Fix the waitlist itself — Makefile, mintest.h and test_clinic.cpp are off-limits, and please don't bolt a re-scan onto the desk-lookup path to hide whatever is going on; lookups by ticket need to stay cheap.
